title: Whitesands Class of 2026 Graduates with Distinction
excerpt: A moving Valedictory Mass and Graduation Ceremony marked the end of an outstanding year — a perfect WAEC pass rate, ten university scholarships, and a culture of brotherhood that has defined this cohort.
content:
The Whitesands Class of 2026 graduated on a bright morning in May, in a ceremony that blended sacred liturgy with the joy of a school sending its young people into the world.

Principal addressed the graduands directly, urging them to carry the school's motto — Duc in Altum — into every room they would step into thereafter. "You are not only leaving with results," he said. "You are leaving with a way of being."

More than a third of the class will go on to read STEM degrees, with placements confirmed at universities in Nigeria, the United Kingdom, Canada and the United States. Ten students received merit scholarships of varying tiers.

The ceremony closed with the traditional procession through the school's main avenue, lined by staff and underclassmen — a quiet, unforgettable image of a community that had walked with these boys for years.

title: Inter-House Athletics: Purple House Crowned Overall Champions
excerpt: Five days of competition, four houses, one trophy. Purple House lifted the 2026 Inter-House Athletics Championship after a thrilling final-day showing in the relays.
content:
The 2026 Inter-House Athletics Championship closed last Friday with Purple House taking the overall trophy — their first title in three years.

The week-long tournament drew the whole school community: parents in house colours, staff on the sidelines, and students who packed the stands from first whistle to last. Track and field events were balanced this year, with Yellow taking sprint honours, Red dominating the throws, and Orange producing breakthrough performances in the middle distances.

Purple's win was sealed in the senior 4×100m relay — a clean, deliberate handover that the visiting alumni called the best they had seen on this track.

Most Valuable Athlete went to an SS3 sprinter whose 100m time set a new school record. The Director of Sports praised the spirit shown across all four houses and reminded students that inter-house sport at Whitesands is, above all, an exercise in character.

title: PARC National Competition: Whitesands Among the Top Finalists
excerpt: Our research team placed in the top tier of the Pan-African Research Challenge — a months-long competition testing analytical thinking, original research, and presentation under pressure.
content:
Three of our senior students represented Whitesands at the national finals of the Pan-African Research Challenge (PARC) this month, placing among the top finalists in a field of more than sixty schools.

Their submission — a study on water access and household behaviour in two Lagos communities — was praised by the judges for both methodological honesty and the clarity of its policy recommendations.

The team was mentored by faculty from the Sciences and Humanities departments, and prepared over five months of weekend research sessions. Their final presentation, delivered in front of a panel that included two professors and a state-level policymaker, drew sustained applause.

Whitesands will return to PARC next year with a new team — and a higher bar.

title: Annual Cultural Day: Nigeria, in All Her Colours
excerpt: From Yoruba talking drums to Igbo masquerade footwork, Hausa fanfare to Edo bronze-craft — our Cultural Day was an act of love for the country we belong to.
content:
Cultural Day at Whitesands is more than a celebration. It is a school's way of saying to its students: know where you come from, and carry it well.

This year's edition filled the school grounds with the sound of three languages, the colour of half a dozen tribes, and food that drew long, patient queues. Year groups had prepared for weeks — the costumes were authentic, the dances rehearsed, the speeches researched.

The Cultural Procession around the school was led by senior students in traditional dress and watched by parents lining the route. Several alumni — now university students — came home for the occasion.

Whitesands is, by design, a Nigerian school. Cultural Day is when that is made loudest.

title: Admissions Open: JS1 and Lateral Entry for 2026/2027
excerpt: JS1 places are now open, with limited lateral entries available for JS2, JS3 and SS1. Entrance assessments will be held in April and May 2026.
content:
Applications for the 2026/2027 academic year are now open at Whitesands School. We are accepting candidates for JS1 and, on a limited basis, for lateral entry into JS2, JS3 and SS1.

Entrance assessments are scheduled for Saturday 25 April and Saturday 9 May 2026. Each assessment covers English Language, Mathematics, and a general aptitude paper. Shortlisted candidates and their parents will be invited for a structured interview with the Admissions team — an honest, two-way conversation about whether the school and the family are right for one another.

Families are warmly invited to our Open Day on 19 April 2026, where parents and prospective students can tour the campus, meet faculty, and ask their questions in person.

To register, visit the Admissions page or write to the Admissions Office. We reply to every enquiry within one working day.

title: Whitesands Orchestra Performs at the End-of-Year Gala
excerpt: A full hall, a forty-piece student orchestra, and a programme that ranged from baroque to contemporary Nigerian composition. The Gala set a new bar for the Music Department.
content:
The Whitesands Orchestra closed its season with a Gala performance on the school grounds that drew families, alumni and music staff from across Lagos.

The programme was deliberately ambitious — Vivaldi alongside a contemporary piece commissioned for the evening, and three traditional Nigerian arrangements that received the loudest applause of the night.

The orchestra is now in its sixth year. It rehearses twice a week, draws from Years 8 to 12, and is led by a faculty conductor with a soft spot for early Baroque. Auditions for next season's intake will open in September.

title: Virtue of the Month: Perseverance — Stories from Our Students
excerpt: Each month, Whitesands names a virtue and asks its students to live it. This term's virtue was Perseverance — and the stories that came back surprised even us.
content:
Each month at Whitesands, the chaplaincy and the pastoral team name a virtue that the whole school is invited to practise — in lessons, on the field, at home. For this term, the virtue was Perseverance.

An SS1 student wrote about rewriting his history essay three times before he was willing to submit it. An SS3 footballer described pushing through a long-running knee injury to finish his last season for the school. A JS1 boy submitted, simply, a journal entry titled "the day I didn't give up on Maths."

These stories were read in assembly, displayed in the atrium, and — in a quieter way than the prize-givings — shaped how the school felt for a month.

Next month's virtue is Honesty.
